item.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay player in NBA, dies at 47
item.description: Jason Collins, the first openly gay player in the NBA, has died at the age of 47, his family announced. Five months ago, Collins revealed he was diagnosed with brain cancer. Nate Burleson reports.

item.title: Trump says he doesn't think about Americans' "financial situation" while Iran war continues
item.description: President Trump addressed the economic woes some Americans are feeling linked to the Iran war. This comes as concerning, new inflation data emerges. Business Insider's Allie Kelly joins with more.

item.title: What are today's HELOC and home equity loan interest rates?
item.description: HELOCs and home equity loans offer homeowners an affordable way to borrow money now. Here are the rates for each.

item.title: What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance interest rates?
item.description: Knowing today's mortgage rates can help new homebuyers and homeowners looking to lock in a good deal.

item.title: What is the price of silver today?
item.description: Silver can play a critical role in a diversified portfolio. Here's how much it costs per ounce right now.

item.title: What is the price of gold today?
item.description: Understanding the pricing trends and dynamics of the gold market is crucial for making sound financial choices.

item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman pushes back against Elon Musk during testimony
item.description: In federal court on Tuesday, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman testified about his leadership and pushed back against claims made by co-founder Elon Musk. Musk, who has since launched his own AI company, is suing Altman and OpenAI, claiming it flipped its original structure as a neutral nonprofit in favor of a for-profit business.

item.title: Trump arrives in China for summit with Xi Jinping | Special Report
item.description: President Trump landed in Beijing, China, for a summit with President Xi Jinping. The historic meeting comes as the Iran war continues and as questions linger about Taiwan's future. Adriana Diaz anchored this CBS News special report.

item.title: Iran insists it's ready for a new U.S. attack as Trump arrives in China
item.description: Iran's military says it's trained and ready for any new U.S. assault as Trump predicts a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

item.title: Video shows rescue of 11 people on board small plane that crashed off Florida coast
item.description: A small plane from the Bahamas crashed into the Atlantic on Tuesday off the coast of Florida. Officials say all 11 people on board the plane were rescued and brought to shore for medical treatment.

item.title: New details on quarantine for Americans who were on hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Eighteen Americans are under observation in medical facilities, including 16 in Nebraska, after they were aboard the cruise ship struck by hantavirus. One American has tested positive for the virus. Ian Lee has more.

item.title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."

item.title: Trump heads to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
item.description: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

item.title: U.S. students' "reading recession" continuing but some places bucking the trend
item.description: Researchers say the U.S. is experiencing a "reading recession" that predates the pandemic. But some places are bucking the trend, chalking up higher test scores.

item.title: Trump promised to hold 30,000 migrants at Guantanamo. It's mostly empty.
item.description: A CBS News review of internal government documents and information provided to Congress shows immigration detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay are nearly empty.

item.title: Judge shot dead while heading to gym without her bodyguards
item.description: Lady Pachar was shot that day while traveling by car to a gym in the southwestern city of Machala.

item.title: Convicted Utah author's son reveals new details about night his dad died
item.description: Prosecutors said Kouri Richins laced her husband's cocktail with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl in 2022.

item.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.

item.title: IDPH investigating possible hantavirus in Winnebago Co. not linked to cruise
item.description: The Illinois Department of Public Health said it is investigating a potential case of hantavirus in an Illinois resident, that they said is not linked to the deadly cruise ship outbreak.

item.title: Watch: Pete Hegseth faces lawmakers' questions as he vies for $1.5 trillion defense budget
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine faced bipartisan questioning on Capitol Hill about the war in Iran on Tuesday. The Trump administration is seeking $1.5 trillion for defense in the 2027 budget proposal, a starting point for negotiations. Hegseth called it "admittedly a historic budget" in his opening statement.

item.title: What to know about the primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
item.description: Voters went to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

item.title: Florida's "Alligator Alcatraz" could close in June, sources say
item.description: The closure comes amid escalating operating costs for the facility, which are now estimated to total nearly $1 billion.

item.title: Highlights from previous Trump-Xi meetings
item.description: Wednesday will mark the seventh meeting between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping. Watch CBS News' coverage of previous meetings between the world leaders through the years.

item.title: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 from brain cancer
item.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday.

item.title: Trump on "the only thing that matters" in dealing with Iran
item.description: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: CPI surged in April as inflation soars to highest level in almost 3 years
item.description: U.S. consumer prices rose in April, fueled by a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Trump administration to name David Venturella as interim ICE chief
item.description: The Trump administration plans to name longtime immigration official David Venturella as the interim head of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, replacing acting director Todd Lyons, a spokesperson and two U.S. officials said.

item.title: 5/12: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump says he's not backing down as Iran war spikes cost of living; California mayor pleads guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China.

item.title: Social Security's cost-of-living adjustment could reach nearly 4% in 2027
item.description: A larger COLA would boost monthly checks for retirees, but also strain Social Security's already depleted trust funds.

item.title: Ukraine and U.S. move toward landmark drone defense deal
item.description: A deal is taking shape for the U.S. and Ukraine to jointly develop and build weapons that have been at the forefront of the wars in both Ukraine and Iran.
